credentials, db\nfrom django.conf import settings\nfrom .models import Donor\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nclass FirebaseService:\n    def __init__(self):\n        # Initialize Firebase only once\n        if not firebase_admin._apps:\n            cred = credentials.Certificate(settings.FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH)\n            firebase_admin.initialize_app(cred, {\n                \'databaseURL\': settings.FIREBASE_DATABASE_URL\n            })\n        self.ref = db.reference(\'donors\')\n\n    def _dict_to_donor(self, donor_id, data):\n        """\n        Convert Firebase dict to Donor instance (not saved to DB).\n        """\n        return Donor(\n            id=donor_id,\n            name=data.get(\'name\', \'\'),\n            blood_type=data.get(\'blood_type\', \'\'),\n            location=data.get(\'location\', \'\'),\n            contact_info=data.get(\'contact_info\', \'\'),\n            last_donation=self._parse_datetime(data.get(\'last_donation\')),\n            is_active=data.get(\'is_active\', True)\n        )\n\n    def _donor_to_dict(self, donor):\n        """\n        Convert Donor instance to dict for Firebase.\n        """\n        return {\n            \'name\': donor.name,\n            \'blood_type\': donor.blood_type,\n            \'location\': donor.location,\n            \'contact_info\': donor.contact_info,\n            \'last_donation\': donor.last_donation.isoformat() if donor.last_donation else \'\',\n            \'is_active\': donor.is_active\n        }\n\n    def _parse_datetime(self, value):\n        if not value:\n            return None\n        try:\n            return datetime.fromisoformat(value)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n    def get_donors(self):\n        """\n        Returns a list of Donor instances from Firebase.\n        """\n        donors_data = self.ref.get()\n        donors = []\n        if donors_data:\n            for donor_id, data in donors_data.items():\n                donors.append(self._dict_to_donor(donor_id, data))\n        return donors\n\n    def get_donor(self, donor_id):\n        """\n        Returns a single Donor instance by donor_id from Firebase.\n        """\n        data = self.ref.child(donor_id).get()\n        if data:\n            return self._dict_to_donor(donor_id, data)\n        return None\n\n    def add_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Adds a new donor to Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).set(donor_dict)\n\n    def update_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Updates an existing donor in Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).update(donor_dict)\n\n    def delete_donor(self, donor_id):\n        """\n        Deletes a donor from Firebase.\n        """\n        self.ref.child(donor_id).delete()'}, Flatten JSON data for CSV export\n            if isinstance(self.data, dict):\n                for key, value in self.data.items():\n                    writer.writerow([key, value])\n            else:\n                writer.writerow([str(self.data)])\n            content = output.getvalue()\n            output.close()\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.csv\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        elif format == \'json\':\n            content = json.dumps(self.data, indent=2)\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.json\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported export format: {}".format(format))\n\n    def __str__(self):\n        return f"Report {self.id} ({self.get_type_display()}) - {self.created_at.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}"'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The Report model for the reporting app has been implemented. timedelta(days=days)\n        donors_qs = Donor.objects.filter(is_active=True)\n        if blood_type:\n            donors_qs = donors_qs.filter(blood_type=blood_type)\n        recent_donors = donors_qs.filter(last_donation__gte=since_date)\n        data = {\n            "report_type": "Donation",\n            "blood_type": blood_type if blood_type else "All",\n            "since_date": since_date.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d\'),\n            "total_donors": donors_qs.count(),\n            "recent_donors": recent_donors.count(),\n            "donors": [\n                {\n                    "id": donor.id,\n                    "name": donor.name,\n                    "blood_type": donor.blood_type,\n                    "location": donor.location,\n                    "last_donation": donor.last_donation.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M\') if donor.last_donation else None,\n                }\n                for donor in recent_donors\n            ]\n        }\n        return data\n\n    elif report_type == \'activity\':\n        # Example: Donor activity in a given period\n        days = int(params.get(\'days\', 30))\n        since_date = timezone.now() - def add_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Adds a new donor to Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).set(donor_dict)\n\n from django.db import models
from django.contrib.auth.models import AbstractUser
from django.utils import timezone

class User(AbstractUser):
    """
    Extends Django's AbstractUser to support authentication and access control.
    Additional fields can be added here if needed for registration or profile.
    """
    # You can add more fields if needed for registration/profile
    # For now, we use the default AbstractUser fields

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.username} ({self.email})"

class PasswordResetToken(models.Model):
    """
    Stores password reset tokens for users.
    """
    user = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='password_reset_tokens')
    token = models.CharField(max_length=128, unique=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now)
    is_used = models.BooleanField(default=False)

    def __str__(self):
        return f"Password reset token for {self.user.username} ({'used' if self.is_used else 'active'})"

And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. Instead, use the Editor.goto_line command. It\'s much quicker. \n2. Always make sure to look at the currently open file and the current working directory (which appears right after the currently open file). The currently open file might be in a different directory than the working directory! Note that some commands, such as \'create\', open files, so they might change the current open file.\n3. When using Editor.edit_file_by_replace, if there is no exact match, take the difference in indentation into consideration.\n4. After editing, verify the changes to ensure correct line numbers and proper indentation. Adhere to PEP8 standards for Python code.\n5. NOTE ABOUT THE EDIT COMMAND: Indentation really matters! When editing a file, make sure to insert appropriate indentation before each line! Ensuring the code adheres to PEP8 standards. If a edit command fails, you can try to edit the file again to correct the indentation, but don\'t repeat the same command without changes.\n6. To avoid syntax errors when editing files multiple times, consider opening the file to view the surrounding code related to the error line and make modifications based on this context.\n7. Ensure to observe the currently open file and the current working directory, which is displayed right after the open file. The open file might be in a different directory than the working directory. Remember, commands like \'create\' open files and might alter the current open file.\n8. Effectively using Use search commands (`search_dir`, `search_file`, `find_file`) and navigation commands (`open_file`, `goto_line`) to locate and modify files efficiently. The Editor tool can fully satisfy the requirements. Follow these steps and considerations for optimal results:\n\n9. When the edit fails, try to enlarge the range of code.\n10. You must use the Editor.open_file command to open a file before using the Editor tool\'s edit command to modify it. When you open a file, any currently open file will be automatically closed.\n11. Remember, when you use Editor.insert_content_at_line or Editor.edit_file_by_replace, the line numbers will change after the operation. Therefore, if there are multiple operations, perform only the first operation in the current response, and defer the subsequent operations to the next turn.\n11.1 Do not use Editor.insert_content_at_line or Editor.edit_file_by_replace more than once per command list.\n12. If you choose Editor.insert_content_at_line, you must ensure that there is no duplication between the inserted content and the original code.           \'name\': donor.name,\n            \'blood_type\': donor.blood_type,\n            \'location\': donor.location,\n            \'contact_info\': donor.contact_info,\n            \'last_donation\': donor.last_donation.isoformat() if donor.last_donation else \'\',\n            \'is_active\': donor.is_active\n        }\n\n    def _parse_datetime(self, value):\n        if not value:\n            return None\n        try:\n            return datetime.fromisoformat(value)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n    def get_donors(self):\n        """\n        Returns a list of Donor instances from Firebase.\n        """\n        donors_data = self.ref.get()\n        donors = []\n        if donors_data:\n            for donor_id, data in donors_data.items():\n                donors.append(self._dict_to_donor(donor_id, data))\n        return donors\n\n    def get_donor(self, donor_id):\n        """\n        Returns a single Donor instance by donor_id from Firebase.\n        """\n        data = self.ref.child(donor_id).get()\n        if data:\n            return self._dict_to_donor(donor_id, data)\n        return None\n\n    def add_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Adds a new donor to Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).set(donor_dict)\n\n    def update_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Updates an existing donor in Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).update(donor_dict)\n\n    def delete_donor(self, donor_id):\n        """\n        Deletes a donor from Firebase.\n        """\n        self.ref.child(donor_id).delete()'}, Flatten JSON data for CSV export\n            if isinstance(self.data, dict):\n                for key, value in self.data.items():\n                    writer.writerow([key, value])\n            else:\n                writer.writerow([str(self.data)])\n            content = output.getvalue()\n            output.close()\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.csv\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        elif format == \'json\':\n            content = json.dumps(self.data, indent=2)\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.json\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported export format: {}".format(format))\n\n    def __str__(self):\n        return f"Report {self.id} ({self.get_type_display()}) - {self.created_at.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}"'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The Report model for the reporting app has been implemented. Integrate the application with Firebase Realtime Database for real-time data synchronization and scalability.\\n016|\\n017|### 2.2 User Stories\\n018|- As a blood bank administrator, I want to manage donor records efficiently so that I can keep the database up to date.\\n019|- As a donor, I want to register and update my information online so that I can be contacted for donations when needed.\\n020|- As a staff member, I want to search and filter donor lists by blood type and location so that I can quickly find suitable donors.\\n021|- As a system admin, I want to monitor system activity and data integrity so that I can ensure compliance and security.\\n022|- As a user, I want the interface and workflows to remain familiar so that I can transition smoothly to the new system.\\n023|\\n024|### 2.3 Competitive Analysis\\n025|\\n026|| Product Name                | Pros                                                      | Cons                                                      |\\n027||----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|\\n028|| OpenBloodBank (PHP)        | Open source, widely used, feature-rich                    | Outdated UI, limited scalability, PHP-based               |\\n029|| BloodBankPro (Django)      | Modern Python stack, good documentation, modular           | Paid license, limited customization                       |\\n030|| DonorConnect (Flask)       | Lightweight, easy to deploy, RESTful API                   | Fewer built-in features, basic UI                         |\\n031|| RedCross Donor Portal      | Trusted brand, robust security, mobile support             | Not open source, high cost, complex integration           |\\n032|| eBloodBank (Java)          | Enterprise-grade, scalable, multi-language support         | Heavyweight, complex setup, Java-based                    |\\n033|| BloodHero (Node.js)        | Real-time updates, modern UI, active community             | Node.js learning curve, less mature for healthcare        |\\n034|| Our Target Product         | Familiar UI, Python-based, Firebase integration, open code | Migration complexity, dependency on Firebase              |\\n035|\\n036|```mermaid\\n037|quadrantChart\\n038|    title "Blood Bank Donor Management Systems Comparison"\\n039| Flatten JSON data for CSV export\n            if isinstance(self.data, dict):\n                for key, value in self.data.items():\n                    writer.writerow([key, value])\n            else:\n                writer.writerow([str(self.data)])\n            content = output.getvalue()\n            output.close()\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.csv\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        elif format == \'json\':\n            content = json.dumps(self.data, indent=2)\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.json\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported export format: {}".format(format))\n\n    def __str__(self):\n        return f"Report {self.id} ({self.get_type_display()}) - {self.created_at.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}"'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The Report model for the reporting app has been implemented. Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/blood_bank_donor_management_python/authentication/views.py has been successfully created, with content:
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect
from django.contrib.auth import authenticate, login, logout
from django.contrib.auth.decorators import login_required
from django.contrib import messages
from django.urls import reverse
from django.utils import timezone
from django.http import HttpResponse, HttpResponseBadRequest
from .models import User, PasswordResetToken
from .forms import LoginForm, RegistrationForm, PasswordResetRequestForm, PasswordResetForm
import uuid

def login_view(request):
    """
    Handle user login.
    """
    if request.user.is_authenticated:
        return redirect('dashboard')
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form = LoginForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            username = form.cleaned_data['username']
            password = form.cleaned_data['password']
            user = authenticate(request, username=username, password=password)
            if user is not None:
                login(request, user)
                messages.success(request, "Login successful.")
                return redirect('dashboard')
            else:
                messages.error(request, "Invalid username or password.")
    else:
        form = LoginForm()
    return render(request, 'login.html', {'form': form})

@login_required
def logout_view(request):
    """
    Handle user logout.
    """
    logout(request)
    messages.success(request, "You have been logged out.")
    return redirect('login')

def register_view(request):
    """
    Handle new user registration.
    """
    if request.user.is_authenticated:
        return redirect('dashboard')
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form = RegistrationForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            user = form.save(commit=False)
            user.set_password(form.cleaned_data['password1'])
            user.save()
            messages.success(request, "Registration successful. Please log in.")
            return redirect('login')
        else:
            messages.error(request, "Please correct the errors below.")
    else:
        form = RegistrationForm()
    return render(request, 'register.html', {'form': form})

def password_reset_request_view(request):
    """
    Handle password reset request (send token).
    """
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form = PasswordResetRequestForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            email = form.cleaned_data['email']
            try:
                user = User.objects.get(email=email)
                token = str(uuid.uuid4())
                PasswordResetToken.objects.create(user=user, token=token, created_at=timezone.now())
                # In production, send token via email. Here, display for demo.
                messages.success(request, f"Password reset token: {token}")
                return redirect('password_reset')
            except User.DoesNotExist:
                messages.error(request, "No user found with that email address.")
        else:
            messages.error(request, "Please correct the errors below.")
    else:
        form = PasswordResetRequestForm()
    return render(request, 'password_reset_request.html', {'form': form})

def password_reset_view(request):
    """
    Handle password reset using token.
    """
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form = PasswordResetForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            token = form.cleaned_data['token']
            password = form.cleaned_data['password1']
            try:
                reset_token = PasswordResetToken.objects.get(token=token, is_used=False)
                user = reset_token.user
                user.set_password(password)
                user.save()
                reset_token.is_used = True
                reset_token.save()
                messages.success(request, "Password reset successful. Please log in.")
                return redirect('login')
            except PasswordResetToken.DoesNotExist:
                messages.error(request, "Invalid or expired token.")
        else:
            messages.error(request, "Please correct the errors below.")
    else:
        form = PasswordResetForm()
    return render(request, 'password_reset.html', {'form': form})

def add_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Adds a new donor to Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).set(donor_dict)\n\n Flatten JSON data for CSV export\n            if isinstance(self.data, dict):\n                for key, value in self.data.items():\n                    writer.writerow([key, value])\n            else:\n                writer.writerow([str(self.data)])\n            content = output.getvalue()\n            output.close()\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.csv\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        elif format == \'json\':\n            content = json.dumps(self.data, indent=2)\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.json\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported export format: {}".format(format))\n\n    def __str__(self):\n        return f"Report {self.id} ({self.get_type_display()}) - {self.created_at.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}"'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The Report model for the reporting app has been implemented. from django import forms
from django.contrib.auth.forms import UserCreationForm, AuthenticationForm
from .models import User

class LoginForm(forms.Form):
    username = forms.CharField(
        max_length=150,
        widget=forms.TextInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'Username'}),
        label="Username"
    )
    password = forms.CharField(
        widget=forms.PasswordInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'Password'}),
        label="Password"
    )

class RegistrationForm(UserCreationForm):
    email = forms.EmailField(
        required=True,
        widget=forms.EmailInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'Email'}),
        label="Email"
    )

    class Meta:
        model = User
        fields = ("username", "email", "password1", "password2")
        widgets = {
            'username': forms.TextInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'Username'}),
        }

    def clean_email(self):
        email = self.cleaned_data.get('email')
        if User.objects.filter(email=email).exists():
            raise forms.ValidationError("A user with that email already exists.")
        return email

class PasswordResetRequestForm(forms.Form):
    email = forms.EmailField(
        required=True,
        widget=forms.EmailInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'Email'}),
        label="Email"
    )

class PasswordResetForm(forms.Form):
    token = forms.CharField(
        max_length=128,
        widget=forms.TextInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'Reset Token'}),
        label="Reset Token"
    )
    password1 = forms.CharField(
        widget=forms.PasswordInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'New Password'}),
        label="New Password"
    )
    password2 = forms.CharField(
        widget=forms.PasswordInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'Confirm New Password'}),
        label="Confirm New Password"
    )

    def clean(self):
        cleaned_data = super().clean()
        password1 = cleaned_data.get("password1")
        password2 = cleaned_data.get("password2")
        if password1 and password2 and password1 != password2:
            self.add_error('password2', "Passwords do not match.")
        return cleaned_data

Example 1: Given a file \\"/workspace/example.txt\\" with the following content: ``` 001|contain f 002|contain g 003|contain h 004|contain i ``` EDITING: If you want to replace line 2 and line 3 edit_file_by_replace( \\"/workspace/example.txt\\", first_replaced_line_number=2, first_replaced_line_content=\\"contain g\\", last_replaced_line_number=3, last_replaced_line_content=\\"contain h\\", new_content=\\"new content\\", ) This will replace the second line 2 and line 3 with \\"new content\\". an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. \'databaseURL\': settings.FIREBASE_DATABASE_URL\n            })\n        self.ref = db.reference(\'donors\')\n\n    def _dict_to_donor(self, donor_id, data):\n        """\n        Convert Firebase           \'name\': donor.name,\n            \'blood_type\': donor.blood_type,\n            \'location\': donor.location,\n            \'contact_info\': donor.contact_info,\n            \'last_donation\': donor.last_donation.isoformat() if donor.last_donation else \'\',\n            \'is_active\': donor.is_active\n        }\n\n    def _parse_datetime(self, value):\n        if not value:\n            return None\n        try:\n            return datetime.fromisoformat(value)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n    def get_donors(self):\n        """\n        Returns a list of Donor instances from Firebase.\n        """\n        donors_data = self.ref.get()\n        donors = []\n        if donors_data:\n            for donor_id, data in donors_data.items():\n                donors.append(self._dict_to_donor(donor_id, data))\n        return donors\n\n    def get_donor(self, donor_id):\n        """\n        Returns a single Donor instance by donor_id from Firebase.\n        """\n        data = self.ref.child(donor_id).get()\n        if data:\n            return self._dict_to_donor(donor_id, data)\n        return None\n\n    def add_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Adds a new donor to Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).set(donor_dict)\n\n    def update_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Updates an existing donor in Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).update(donor_dict)\n\n    def delete_donor(self, donor_id):\n        """\n        Deletes a donor from Firebase.\n        """\n        self.ref.child(donor_id).delete()'}, timedelta(days=days)\n        donors_qs = Donor.objects.filter(is_active=True)\n        if blood_type:\n            donors_qs = donors_qs.filter(blood_type=blood_type)\n        recent_donors = donors_qs.filter(last_donation__gte=since_date)\n        data = {\n            "report_type": "Donation",\n            "blood_type": blood_type if blood_type else "All",\n            "since_date": since_date.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d\'),\n            "total_donors": donors_qs.count(),\n            "recent_donors": recent_donors.count(),\n            "donors": [\n                {\n                    "id": donor.id,\n                    "name": donor.name,\n                    "blood_type": donor.blood_type,\n                    "location": donor.location,\n                    "last_donation": donor.last_donation.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M\') if donor.last_donation else None,\n                }\n                for donor in recent_donors\n            ]\n        }\n        return data\n\n    elif report_type == \'activity\':\n        # Example: Donor activity in a given period\n        days = int(params.get(\'days\', 30))\n        since_date = timezone.now() - def add_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Adds a new donor to Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).set(donor_dict)\n\n    def update_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Updates an existing donor in Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n credentials, db\nfrom django.conf import settings\nfrom .models import Donor\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nclass FirebaseService:\n    def __init__(self):\n        # Initialize Firebase only once\n        if not firebase_admin._apps:\n            cred = credentials.Certificate(settings.FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH)\n            firebase_admin.initialize_app(cred, {\n                \'databaseURL\': settings.FIREBASE_DATABASE_URL\n            })\n        self.ref = db.reference(\'donors\')\n\n    def _dict_to_donor(self, donor_id, data):\n        """\n        Convert Firebase dict to Donor instance (not saved to DB).\n        """\n        return Donor(\n            id=donor_id,\n            name=data.get(\'name\', \'\'),\n            blood_type=data.get(\'blood_type\', \'\'),\n            location=data.get(\'location\', \'\'),\n            contact_info=data.get(\'contact_info\', \'\'),\n            last_donation=self._parse_datetime(data.get(\'last_donation\')),\n            is_active=data.get(\'is_active\', True)\n        )\n\n    def _donor_to_dict(self, donor):\n        """\n        Convert Donor instance to dict for Firebase.\n        """\n        return {\n            \'name\': donor.name,\n            \'blood_type\': donor.blood_type,\n            \'location\': donor.location,\n            \'contact_info\': donor.contact_info,\n            \'last_donation\': donor.last_donation.isoformat() if donor.last_donation else \'\',\n            \'is_active\': donor.is_active\n        }\n\n    def _parse_datetime(self, value):\n        if not value:\n            return None\n        try:\n            return datetime.fromisoformat(value)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n    def get_donors(self):\n        """\n        Returns a list of Donor instances from Firebase.\n        """\n        donors_data = self.ref.get()\n        donors = []\n        if donors_data:\n            for donor_id, data in donors_data.items():\n                donors.append(self._dict_to_donor(donor_id, data))\n        return donors\n\n    def get_donor(self, donor_id):\n        """\n        Returns a single Donor instance by donor_id from Firebase.\n        """\n        data = self.ref.child(donor_id).get()\n        if data:\n            return self._dict_to_donor(donor_id, data)\n        return None\n\n    def add_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Adds a new donor to Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).set(donor_dict)\n\n    def update_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Updates an existing donor in Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).update(donor_dict)\n\n    def delete_donor(self, donor_id):\n        """\n        Deletes a donor from Firebase.\n        """\n        self.ref.child(donor_id).delete()'}, Flatten JSON data for CSV export\n            if isinstance(self.data, dict):\n                for key, value in self.data.items():\n                    writer.writerow([key, value])\n            else:\n                writer.writerow([str(self.data)])\n            content = output.getvalue()\n            output.close()\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.csv\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        elif format == \'json\':\n            content = json.dumps(self.data, indent=2)\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.json\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported export format: {}".format(format))\n\n    def __str__(self):\n        return f"Report {self.id} ({self.get_type_display()}) - {self.created_at.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}"'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The Report model for the reporting app has been implemented. timedelta(days=days)\n        donors_qs = Donor.objects.filter(is_active=True)\n        if blood_type:\n            donors_qs = donors_qs.filter(blood_type=blood_type)\n        recent_donors = donors_qs.filter(last_donation__gte=since_date)\n        data = {\n            "report_type": "Donation",\n            "blood_type": blood_type if blood_type else "All",\n            "since_date": since_date.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d\'),\n            "total_donors": donors_qs.count(),\n            "recent_donors": recent_donors.count(),\n            "donors": [\n                {\n                    "id": donor.id,\n                    "name": donor.name,\n                    "blood_type": donor.blood_type,\n                    "location": donor.location,\n                    "last_donation": donor.last_donation.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M\') if donor.last_donation else None,\n                }\n                for donor in recent_donors\n            ]\n        }\n        return data\n\n    elif report_type == \'activity\':\n        # Example: Donor activity in a given period\n        days = int(params.get(\'days\', 30))\n        since_date = timezone.now() - Flatten JSON data for CSV export\n            if isinstance(self.data, dict):\n                for key, value in self.data.items():\n                    writer.writerow([key, value])\n            else:\n                writer.writerow([str(self.data)])\n            content = output.getvalue()\n            output.close()\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.csv\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        elif format == \'json\':\n            content = json.dumps(self.data, indent=2)\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.json\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported export format: {}".format(format))\n\n    def __str__(self):\n        return f"Report {self.id} ({self.get_type_display()}) - {self.created_at.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}"'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The Report model for the reporting app has been implemented. value:\n            return None\n        try:\n            return datetime.fromisoformat(value)\n        except Exception:\n            return None\n\n    def get_donors(self):\n        """\n        Returns a list of Donor instances from Firebase.\n        """\n        donors_data = self.ref.get()\n        donors = []\n        if donors_data:\n            for donor_id, data in def add_donor(self, donor):\n        """\n        Adds a new donor to Firebase.\n        """\n        donor_dict = self._donor_to_dict(donor)\n        self.ref.child(donor.id).set(donor_dict)\n\n Flatten JSON data for CSV export\n            if isinstance(self.data, dict):\n                for key, value in self.data.items():\n                    writer.writerow([key, value])\n            else:\n                writer.writerow([str(self.data)])\n            content = output.getvalue()\n            output.close()\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.csv\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        elif format == \'json\':\n            content = json.dumps(self.data, indent=2)\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.json\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported export format: {}".format(format))\n\n    def __str__(self):\n        return f"Report {self.id} ({self.get_type_display()}) - {self.created_at.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}"'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The Report model for the reporting app has been implemented. Example: Aggregate donation data\n            self.data = {\n                "summary": "Donation report generated.",\n                "params": params\n            }\n        elif self.type == \'activity\':\n            self.data = {\n                "summary": "Donor activity report generated.",\n                "params": params\n            }\n        elif self.type == \'inventory\':\n            self.data = {\n                "summary": "Inventory report generated.",\n                "params": params\n            }\n        else:\n            self.data = {\n                "summary": "Custom report generated.",\n                "params": params\n            }\n        self.save()\n        return self\n\n    def export(self, format: str):\n        """\n        Export the report data in the specified format.\n        Supported formats: \'csv\', \'json\'\n        Returns a Django File object.\n        """\n        if format == \'csv\':\n            output = io.StringIO()\n            writer = csv.writer(output)\n            # Flatten JSON data for CSV export\n            if isinstance(self.data, dict):\n                for key, value in self.data.items():\n                    writer.writerow([key, value])\n            else:\n                writer.writerow([str(self.data)])\n            content = output.getvalue()\n            output.close()\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.csv\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        elif format == \'json\':\n            content = json.dumps(self.data, indent=2)\n            filename = f\'report_{self.id}.json\'\n            return ContentFile(content, name=filename)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError("Unsupported export format: {}".format(format))\n\n    def __str__(self):\n        return f"Report {self.id} ({self.get_type_display()}) - {self.created_at.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}"'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The Report model for the reporting app has been implemented. timedelta(days=days)\n        donors_qs = Donor.objects.filter(is_active=True)\n        if blood_type:\n            donors_qs = donors_qs.filter(blood_type=blood_type)\n        recent_donors = donors_qs.filter(last_donation__gte=since_date)\n        data = {\n            "report_type": "Donation",\n            "blood_type": blood_type if blood_type else "All",\n            "since_date": since_date.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d\'),\n            "total_donors": donors_qs.count(),\n            "recent_donors": recent_donors.count(),\n            "donors": [\n                {\n                    "id": donor.id,\n                    "name": donor.name,\n                    "blood_type": donor.blood_type,\n                    "location": donor.location,\n                    "last_donation": donor.last_donation.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M\') if donor.last_donation else None,\n                }\n                for donor in recent_donors\n            ]\n        }\n        return data\n\n    elif report_type == \'activity\':\n        # Example: Donor activity in a given period\n        days = int(params.get(\'days\', 30))\n        since_date = timezone.now() -
